data.title: As a project onboarder, I get CI config keys populated from project detection
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend onboarding Phase 3 Step 2b config template to include CI detection-backed keys (ci.fast_gate_job, ci.fast_fail_job, ci.test_ceil_job, ci.integration_workflow) populated from project-detect.sh output. Add a numbered selection dialogue when ci_workflow_confidence=low or ci_workflow_names contains 2+ entries; skip CI question when ci_workflow_confidence=high and exactly one entry.
**Why**: The CI autodetection wizard was dropped during the project-setup→onboarding merge, leaving these keys missing from generated dso-config.conf files.
**Scope**:
- IN: SKILL.md Phase 3 Step 2b config template extension for 4 CI keys; Socratic dialogue with numbered selection gated on ci_workflow_confidence; ci.integration_workflow populated from ci_workflow_names
- OUT: Changes to project-detect.sh detection capabilities; new CI keys beyond the 4 specified; changes to validate-config.sh KNOWN_KEYS (already present)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, onboarding Phase 3 Step 2b generates ci.fast_gate_job, ci.fast_fail_job, ci.test_ceil_job, and ci.integration_workflow in dso-config.conf, populated from project-detect.sh output and confirmed by the user
  ← Satisfies: "SC1 — detection-backed keys in config template"
- When this story is complete, projects with 2+ CI workflow files or ci_workflow_confidence=low see a numbered selection of detected workflow filenames (not free-text), and projects with a single high-confidence workflow get no CI clarification question
  ← Satisfies: "SC2 — CI Socratic dialogue gated on confidence"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] ci_workflow_confidence may be absent from project-detect.sh output on projects without CI — default to "low" (ask)
- [Maintainability] All SKILL.md stories modify the same file — sequential execution recommended to avoid merge conflicts
